Four-time Olympian swimmerEmily Seebhomhas revealed the Olympic village is “like Tinder” despite the “anti-sex beds” attempting to deter athlete intimacy in Paris.

The 32-year-old Aussie champion dished out raunchy details on what happens behind the scenes at theOlympic villageand called out the biggest “root rats” as this year’s Games is in full swing in the City of Love.

Despite the International Olympic Committee organising single beds that feel like cardboard for the athlete’s apartments in a bid to discourage sexual activities, Seebhom said uncomfortable beds wouldn’t stop aroused sports stars.

“No joke it’s probably like Tinder in there,” she told KIIS 97.3’s Robin & Kip on Wednesday morning in an interview about missing out on this year’s Olympics.

When asked who the biggest “root rats” were, Seebohm said: “Oh, God. Um, that’s really hard. If you’re competing later on, you know, you only have a small window to enjoy that.

“Whereas if you’re competing day one or day two and that’s it, it’s all over. So, you sort of can get on it for a bit longer. I don’t know.”

Seebohm insisted that she never got involved with the raunchy antics in the village as she has always been in a long-term relationship with Ryan Gallagher.

The triple Olympic gold medallist also discussed her disappointment at missing out on the 2024 Olympics after she fell short in the trials, just months after giving birth to her son Sampson in September.

She was devastated after failing to qualify in the women’s 100m and 200m backstroke final in the trials.

Seebohm took to X this week to share her devastation.

“I would love to be in Paris right now. Either swimming myself, watching or commentating but the best thing is I’m ok that I’m not there too,” she said.

On Monday, Seebhom battled chilly conditions in Longreach, Queensland, at 3am as she joined locals cheering on the Matildas who went on to defeat Zambia 6-5.

Seebohm, who made her Olympic debut at age 16 in the Beijing 2008 Games, has won three gold, three silver and one bronze medal in her four Olympic appearances.

Before the trials, Seebohm said she was making her Olympic comeback in the hope of inspiring other mothers as well as her son.

“Mums have reached out to me saying how inspiring this is,” she shared.

“When I was pregnant, I felt like I lost the athlete I was.

“This is me trying to regain that and prove to other women that it’s possible to have kids and achieve your dreams too.

“I’m doing this for myself and to prove to Sampson that if you have a dream, don’t let anyone tell you that you can’t do it,’ she went on.

“A lot of people doubted my comeback. I breastfed for eight months, went to training, pumped, went to the gym, and made it to Sampson’s lessons. It was hard, but doable.”
title: Emily Seebohm says Paris Olympics 2024 village is ‘like Tinder’ | PerthNow
description: A decorated Olympian swimmer has revealed the Olympic village is ‘like Tinder’ despite the ‘anti-sex beds’ attempting to deter athlete intimacy in Paris.
